# Pitch Atlas

> A living field manual for how pitchers grip and shape a baseball. Grip first,
> then the physics. Every claim is labeled by its source, not declared right or
> wrong. Principle: "Sourced, not corrected. Many ways can work."

Pitch Atlas (https://pitch-atlas.com/) is a static, single-page reference. The
interactive specimen renders with JavaScript, but the substance is plain text:
grips, release feel, movement physics, master variants, and a full source list.

## How the record is built

Three layers per pitch:

- Foundation: the canonical grip, release, and physics, in plain language. The
  part most coaches agree on.
- Masters: how named arms actually throw it. Each figure is season-stamped and
  confidence-labeled, one click from its source.
- Field Notes: the live community layer. Anyone can post a field note
  anonymously, mark notes tried or helpful, and claim a handle by email; notes
  rank by evidence and context, not by who shouts loudest. Counts come from the
  database, never fabricated.

## Confidence labels

Every visible number carries a provenance label: official-data (Statcast / MLB),
reputable-analysis, secondhand-attributed, pitcher's-own-words, coach-observed,
community-firsthand, or unverified. A leading "approximate" sign marks a real but
rounded, era-dependent, or methodology-bound figure. Nothing is faked; freshness
is computed from when sources were last checked (as of 2026-06-04), never
hardcoded.

## Pitch specimens

- Four-seam fastball: the straightest, fastest pitch; backspin carry.
- Two-seam fastball (sinker): tails arm-side and sinks; a ground-ball pitch.
- Circle changeup: fastball look, arrives a beat slower; wrecks timing.
- 12-6 curveball: topspin drop, twelve o'clock to six.
- Slider: late, short glove-side break; the hitter sees a red dot.

## Source families cited

MLB.com Statcast Glossary, Baseball Savant (Statcast), Driveline Baseball,
FanGraphs, The Hardball Times, Pitcher List, Wikipedia, eFastball, and Plate
Crate, among others. Grip prose is paraphrased and cited, never copied. No MLB,
team, or player photos, logos, footage, or likenesses are used; all geometry and
diagrams are original.
